parameters.query:
INSERT INTO job_log (jobId, type, payload, logged_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 1 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Stamp & Shape Job

### Stamp & Shape Job

Shapes the item: writes `jobId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.jobId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Route by Work Type · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: standard, priority, batch + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → PostgreSQL · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Fast-Track 'batch' + Alert · ④→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → PostgreSQL

Runs a parameterized `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT` upsert — parameters are bound via `queryReplacement`, never string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Fast-Track 'batch' + Alert

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Job Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Job Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Microsoft Teams · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

# Orchestrate a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and PostgreSQL

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Orchestrate a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and PostgreSQL. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual process orchestration is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Stamp & Shape Job → Payload Valid? → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → PostgreSQL → Merge Routed Work → Stamp Completion Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating process orchestration in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Scales without extra headcount
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Postgres connection.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your process orchestration process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
